title: handle_slash_command is a 90-arm dispatch with cyclomatic complexity well above 15
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
complexity
## Location
src/cli/repl/slash.rs:714-807
## Violation
A single 94-line match arm dispatches ~40+ literal command strings (plus aliases). Estimating V(G) = E − N + 2P from the arms alone (each arm + inner alias OR-chain + the colon-plugin fallback): >40 decision points, so V(G) ≫ 15 — far over the rejection threshold. The function also mixes responsibilities: argument parsing, side-effecting handlers, default-result production, and an unknown-command fallback that itself contains a nested colon-split parser. Quoted lines:

    pub fn handle_slash_command(
        input: &str,
        messages: &mut Vec<serde_json::Value>,
        provider: &str,
        current_model: &str,
    ) -> Option<SlashCommandResult> {
        ...
        match cmd.as_str() {
            "help" | "?" => { slash_help(); Some(SlashCommandResult::Handled) }
            "new" | "clear" => { messages.clear(); ... }
            "sessions" | "list" => Some(slash_sessions()),
            ...
            _ => {
                if cmd.contains(':') {
                    let colon_parts: Vec<&str> = cmd.splitn(2, ':').collect();
                    ...

Adding a new command means editing this central switch — OCP violation. Every test of one branch drags in the whole table.
## Mandated refactor
Replace the match with a dispatch registry: `static COMMANDS: &[(&[&str], fn(&str, &mut Vec<Value>, ...) -> SlashCommandResult)]` (or a Vec<Box<dyn SlashCommand>> trait-object registry). Each command is its own struct implementing `name() -> &'static [&'static str]` and `handle(args, ctx) -> SlashCommandResult`. The unknown-command fallback becomes a final entry. Move plugin-colon parsing into its own resolver consulted only after the registry misses. This drops V(G) of the dispatcher to the count of registry-construction lines (mostly straight-line code) and lets each command be unit-tested in isolation.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
